ASBESTOS LAW AND MOTION 503, DEFENDANT CALAVERAS ASBESTOS, LTD.'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION. THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING - DEFENDANT CALAVERAS ASBESTOS, LTD.'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION OF PLAINTIFF'S CLAIM FOR PUNITIVE DAMAGES IS DENIED AS IMPROPERLY SET WITHIN 30 DAYS OF TRIAL IN VIOLATION OF C.C.P. 437C(A). DEFENDANT SET ITS MOTION WITHIN 30 DAYS OF TRIAL WITHOUT AN ORDER FROM THE COURT ALLOWING IT TO DO SO. PLAINTIFF'S OBJECTION TO DEFENDANT'S IMPROPERLY SET MOTION IS SUSTAINED. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: TERI L. JACKSON, REPORTER: KIM RICHARDSON OF AIKEN WELCH, CSR #5915

MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION
Off Calendar Jun-25-2013 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
DEFENDANT CALAVERAS ASBESTOS, LTD.'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION.

APPEARANCES: Jennifer L. Alesio, Esq. of Brayton Purcell LLP 415/898-1555 for plaintiff & moving party; no other appearances.

RULING: The Court adopted its tentative ruling - Defendant Calaveras Asbestos, Ltd.'s motion for summary adjudication of plaintiff's claim for punitive damages is denied as improperly set within 30 days of trial in violation of C.C.P. 437c(a). Defendant set its motion within 30 days of trial without an order from the court allowing it to do so. Plaintiff's objection to defendant's improperly set motion is sustained. Order signed in open court. Judge: Teri L. Jackson, Reporter: Kim Richardson of AIKEN WELCH, CSR #5915 (510/451-1580) (503/TLJ)
